summary:
Technical success during hepatic arterial embolization—including accurate catheter positioning, complete embolic delivery, and achievement of vascular stasis—is closely associated with better prognostic outcomes such as tumor downstaging and extended survival. Significant variation in downstaging rates (between 0% and 50%) underscores how crucial procedural accuracy is to predicting patient response and long-term results.

The summary above contains more than two sentences; please revise it strictly within exactly two sentences while preserving all essential information:

Technical success in hepatic arterial embolization—marked by appropriate catheter placement, sufficient embolic material delivery, and reliable vascular stasis—is strongly linked to favorable outcomes including tumor downstaging and increased overall survival. Variation in downstaging rates ranging from 0% to 50% highlights the significant impact of technical performance on patient prognosis.
